title: Did Jesus Say ‘I Am God; Worship Me’?
primary_keyword: did Jesus say I am God worship me
secondary_keywords: did Jesus claim to be God
where did Jesus say he is God
topic_cluster: Christology and the divine identity of Jesus
search_intent: A Christian, Muslim friend, seeker or sceptic wants to know whether Jesus personally claimed divine identity and accepted worship.
primary_search_query: did Jesus say I am God worship me
secondary_query_family: did Jesus claim to be God
where does Jesus say he is God
what did Jesus mean by I am
answer_system_quote_target: Jesus did not use the exact modern sentence ‘I am God; worship me.’ Yet He claimed divine identity by forgiving sins, using God’s ‘I am’ language, sharing the Father’s honour and glory, and receiving worship. His hearers understood the force of those claims, and the New Testament presents Him as truly divine.
likely_follow_up_questions: Why didn’t Jesus use those exact words?
What did Jesus mean by ‘I am’?
Does Son of God mean Jesus is divine?
app_conversion_path: Invite Christians to practise a clear, Scripture-led answer about Jesus’ divine identity and graciously discuss the key Gospel passages.
cluster_role: Creates a high-intent Christology pillar linking the existing Son of God and Trinity pages.
search_trend_evidence: Fresh search results on 20 July 2026 showed several exact-match and near-exact-match pages for this wording, confirming durable demand. Results often answer combatively or depend on long lists, leaving room for a concise, text-led and gracious explanation.
duplicate_check: Passed against blog/index.html, sitemap.xml, llms.txt and all prior content-plan records. The nearest pages explain the Trinity and the title Son of God; neither answers whether Jesus claimed divine identity through His words, works, authority and acceptance of worship.
readability_check: Short sentences, plain words, active voice and brief paragraphs support the grade 9 or lower target.
editorial_note: No standalone FAQ section or FAQPage schema. Uses the current canonical static article template.
